title: prunus avium f. mamillaris
intro: Prunus avium f. mamillaris, also known as the wild cherry or dwarf cherry, is a variation of the Prunus avium species characterized by its small size and distinctive mamillate (dot-like) leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Prunus avium f. mamillaris include relief from fever, pain, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves, bark, or fruit in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
